channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Rockin’ around the congressional Christmas tree
item.description: Although the House of Representatives rejected the 1,547-page interim spending bill, lawmakers reached an agreement Friday to avoid a government shutdown before the holidays.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump taps 'Apprentice' producer, Mark Burnett, as special envoy to the United Kingdom
item.description: Mark Burnett, the power producer who helped reintroduce Donald Trump to a national television audience with “The Apprentice,” is being tapped by the president-elect as special envoy to the United Kingdom in his upcoming administration.
item.dc:creator: Will Weissert, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Potential Vance Senate replacement travels to Mar-a-Lago as speculation on filling seat intensifies
item.description: Fox News Digital has confirmed that Ohio Lt. Gov. Jon Husted, a candidate to replace JD Vance in the Senate, recently traveled to President-elect Trump's Mar-a-Lago residence.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Senate review of Supreme Court ethics finds more luxury trips and urges enforceable code of conduct
item.description: A nearly two-year investigation by Democratic senators of Supreme Court ethics details more luxury travel by Justice Clarence Thomas and urges Congress to establish a way to enforce a new code of conduct.
item.dc:creator: Lindsay Whitehurst, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: 2025 showdown: This Republican woman may become nation's first Black female governor
item.description: Republican Lt. Gov. Winsome Sears of Virginia could make history next year as the nation's first Black woman to win election as a governor and as the state's first female governor.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Senate approves 235th judge of Biden's term, beating Trump's tally
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden secured the 235th judicial confirmation of his presidency Friday, an accomplishment that exceeds his predecessor's total by one after Democrats put extra emphasis on the federal courts following Donald Trump's far-reaching first term, when he filled three seats
item.dc:creator: KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The 34 House Republicans who voted against a bill to avert a partial government shutdown
item.description: Thirty-four House Republicans voted Friday against a spending bill to avoid a partial government shutdown, while no Democrats opposed the legislation.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: White House pressed on Biden refusing to speak publicly ahead of shutdown
item.description: The White House refused to discuss President Biden’s lack of public appearances amid the ongoing spending bill crisis as a government shutdown looms.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Democrats say lines of communication open with GOP on funding, but no bill presented
item.description: House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries (D-N.Y.) told his party that lines of communication have reopened between his office and Speaker Mike Johnson’s (R-La.) to avert a government shutdown this weekend, members said.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: Pentagon warns that a government shutdown will cost troops their pay over the holidays
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Republicans’ decision to abandon a congressional spending plan will cost about 2.1 million troops their paychecks over the holidays unless some agreement is reached before Friday's deadline to prevent a government shutdown, the Pentagon warned.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR and TARA COPP, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: House Republicans reach new plan to avoid government shutdown after back-to-back defeats
item.description: House Republicans have tentatively reached another deal to avert a partial government shutdown, as the clock ticks closer to midnight.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Government shutdown is averted just after deadline as Congress rejects Trump's debt limit demands
item.description: The Senate has rushed through final passage of a bipartisan plan to fund federal operations and prevent a government shutdown.
item.dc:creator: Lisa Mascaro, Farnoush Amiri And Matt Brown, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Biden canceling student loan debt for 55K public service workers
item.description: President Biden announced on Friday he would be canceling more student debt for tens of thousands of public service workers, possibly his last major move on the issue before leaving office in a month.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump’s reelection victory pushes some LGTBQ Minneapolis residents to safety training
item.description: For some marginalized communities the second Trump term is fraught with fears over personal safety. In Minneapolis, one organization is helping with de-escalation services and gun training.
item.dc:creator: Odette Yousef, MPR News

item.title: Minnesota hastens to regulate helium mining after deposit discovery near Babbitt
item.description: Minnesota is scrambling to figure out how to regulate a potential new industry — one discovered by accident — that is both extremely valuable and in demand.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer and Gracie Stockton, MPR News

item.title: Republicans from hurricane-ravaged states warn against stripping disaster aid from spending bill
item.description: Senate Republicans from regions affected by hurricanes and other storms are warning negotiators against stripping disaster funding from the stopgap spending bill that is suddenly in turmoil after President-elect Trump heavily criticized Speaker Mike Johnson’s (R-La.) plan.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Jeffries suggests Democrats will oppose a 'clean' funding bill after Johnson's plan falters
item.description: House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries (D-N.Y.) suggested Wednesday that Democrats will oppose any federal spending bill that strays from the bipartisan deal announced a day prior, accusing GOP leaders of reneging on the agreement and risking a government shutdown.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, NewsNation

item.title: Donald Trump said he wants to ban trans athletes from competing. The reality is more nuanced
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump’s campaign promise to ‘ban’ transgender athletes struck a chord with voters. It’s uncertain, however, what exactly Trump will do once his second term begins in January.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Ryan Routh, alleged would-be Trump assassin, faces new felony charge
item.description: Florida Attorney General Ashley Moody obtained an arrest warrant for Ryan Routh on an unrelated charge of attempted felony murder involving a six-year-old in a car accident.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: Former top aide to Mayor Adams expected in court on criminal charges: sources
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A former top aide to New York City Mayor Eric Adams is expected in court Thursday to face corruption charges in the latest blow to an administration beset by searches, resignations and the mayor's own indictment.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Government funding plan collapses as Trump makes new demands
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump has abruptly rejected a bipartisan plan to prevent a Christmastime government shutdown, instead telling House Speaker Mike Johnson and Republicans to essentially renegotiate — days before a deadline when federal funding runs out.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Supreme Court will hear arguments over the law that could ban TikTok in the U.S. if it's not sold
item.description: The justices said Wednesday they will hear arguments Jan. 10 about whether the law impermissibly restricts speech in violation of the First Amendment.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: In disputed Shakopee-area legislative race, voters share what was on uncounted ballots
item.description: While 20 ballots believed to have been discarded before being counted won’t be recovered, some voters who cast those missing ballots shared what was on them. They appear to show Rep. Brad Tabke’s narrow lead would stand up.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Trump tariff plan threatens to drive up gas prices
item.description: Trump said that on his first day in office, he would place 25 percent tariffs on all Canadian and Mexican goods. The two countries are the leading sources of U.S. oil imports.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, Fox 5

item.title: New York judge says Trump is not immune from hush money conviction
item.description: A spokesperson for President-elect Trump said the case should never have been brought, called for its immediate dismissal.
item.dc:creator: Ximena Bustillo, MPR News

item.title: House bill would ban 'unnecessary' genital surgeries for intersex foster youth
item.description: States receiving federal funding for foster care and adoption assistance programs would be required to prohibit certain surgeries for foster children born with intersex traits under legislation introduced Monday by Rep. Sylvia Garcia (D-Texas).
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, Fox 5

item.title: Biden establishes a national monument for Frances Perkins, the 1st female Cabinet secretary
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden on Monday established a national monument honoring the late FDR-era Labor Secretary Frances Perkins, the first woman to serve in a presidential Cabinet and a driving force behind Franklin D. Roosevelt's New Deal programs after the Great Depression.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, AAMER MADHANI and DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Donald Trump says he would consider pardoning NYC Mayor Adams
item.description: NEW YORK CITY (PIX11) -- President-elect Donald Trump on Monday said he would consider pardoning Mayor Eric Adams after his historic indictment on federal charges. "I think that he was treated pretty unfairly," Trump said at a press conference in Florida. "I would certainly look at it.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, PIX 11

item.title: Report documents family separations during 1st Trump term
item.description: A report examining years of family separation policies enacted under the Trump administration is being released Monday by several migrant advocacy nonprofit organizations to remind the public and lawmakers of what transpired, Border Report has learned.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, Fox 5

item.title: Trump claims a 'massive' mandate, but presidents often overread their victories
item.description: There's a long history of presidents claiming that their win means the will of the people is behind their agendas. But elections are rarely full-throated endorsements of a candidate's every policy.
item.dc:creator: Domenico Montanaro, MPR News
